zh.lifeSummary: 當Wiliam David Lynn於1672年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 多聶加爾多聶加爾時，他的父親David William Lynn Linn27歲，母親Janet Thompson36歲。他和Anna Maria Margareta Brand至少有1個女兒。他去世於1727年在美國維吉尼亞州斯波希爾瓦尼亞福雷德利可斯包格享年55歲。

en.Of Loch Lynn, Scotland: Loch Lynn, Cape Province, South Africa

nameMeanings.name: Lynn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhloinn and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn ).

English: habitational name from any of several places so called in Norfolk, in particular King's Lynn, an important center of the medieval wool trade. The placename is probably from an Old Welsh word cognate with Gaelic linn ‘pool, stream’.

variants.name: Lyn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Ding, Fatt, Tov, Yol, Yun.
Scottish and English: variant of   Lynn  .
Probably also an altered form of Dutch Van der Lijn (see Vanderlinden ).

variants.name: Linn
meanings: German: habitational name from a place called Linn, named with lin ‘swamp, bog, marsh’.
German: from an ancient Germanic personal name, Linto, formed with Old High German lind(i) ‘soft’.

variants.name: Lenn
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Lynn  .

variants.name: Flynn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Connor, Declan, Kieran, Aidan, Brigid, Cathal, Dermot, Donal, Eamon, John Patrick, Kiernan.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Floinn ‘descendant of Flann’, a byname meaning ‘red(dish), ruddy’. There were families of this name in various parts of Ireland. This name has also been Anglicized as Lindsay .
English: from Middle English Flin, representing the Old Norse male personal name Fleinn.

variants.name: Lynne
meanings: Scottish or English: variant of   Lynn  .

nameMeanings.name: Wiliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

historicEvents.description: Over 6,000 Irish boys and women sold as slaves since England gained control of Jamaica.
William of Orange (William III) lands at Carrickfergus and defeats James II at Battle of the Boyne.
Exclusion of Catholics from Parliament and all professions.
Catholics hold just 7% of land in Ireland.

en.lifeSummary: When Wiliam David Lynn was born in 1672, in Donegal, County Donegal, Ireland, his father, David William Lynn Linn, was 27 and his mother, Janet Thompson, was 36. He had at least 1 daughter with Anna Maria Margareta Brand. He died in 1727, in Fredericksburg, Spotsylvania, Virginia, United States, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wiliam David Lynn est né en 1672, à Donegal, County Donegal, Irlande, son père, David William Lynn Linn, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Janet Thompson, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Anna Maria Margareta Brand. Il est décédé en 1727, à Fredericksburg, Spotsylvania, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wiliam David Lynn 1672 in Donegal, County Donegal, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater David William Lynn Linn 27 und seine Mutter Janet Thompson 36. Er hatte mit Anna Maria Margareta Brand mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1727 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Fredericksburg, Spotsylvania, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Wiliam David Lynnが1672年にアイルランドコウントイ ドンエガルドンエガルで生まれた時，父David William Lynn Linnは27歳，母Janet Thompsonは36歳であった。彼はAnna Maria Margareta Brandとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1727年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州スプオトシルヴァニアフレデリクスブルグで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Wiliam David Lynn(이)가 1672년 아일랜드 카운티 도네걸 도네걸에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David William Lynn Linn(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Thompson(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Anna Maria Margareta Brand(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1727년 미국 버지니아 주 스포츠이반이아 프레더릭크스버그에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.
